title: Agent Coordination Primitives for todo.open

current_model.concurrency_control: version integer (increments on mutation); no ETag, no optimistic locking
agent_coordination_gaps: No atomic claim/acquire semantics — two agents can race to PATCH status=in_progress
No lock expiry or heartbeat — claimed tasks stay claimed forever if agent dies
No assigned_agent field (assignee is a free string, not a machine identity)
No dependency/trigger chain — parent_id is purely hierarchical, not 'trigger next task'
No completion handoff — no way to say 'done, spawn task B'
No event/watch stream — agents must poll to discover available tasks
No idempotency keys — agents can't safely retry writes

beans_prior_art.concurrency_control: ETag on every bean (FNV-1a hash of rendered Markdown content). PATCH requires If-Match header. Server returns ETagMismatchError on conflict. Optional require_if_match mode.
beans_prior_art.watch_stream: Subscribe() channel API with fsnotify + 100ms debounce. Fan-out to multiple subscribers. Events: created/updated/deleted.
beans_prior_art.agent_integration: beans prime command injected at SessionStart and PreCompact hooks. Agent reads task list, checks blocking/blocked_by relationships. No claim/lock primitives — relies on agent discipline.
beans_prior_art.relationships: blocking[], blocked_by[] arrays on each bean. Hierarchical parent field.
gaps_for_multi_agent: No atomic claim — two Claude instances could both grab the same task
No heartbeat/expiry — no TTL on in-progress tasks

patterns_reviewed.name: Celery / task queue pattern
key_primitives: Atomic DEQUEUE: tasks are atomically popped from queue (Redis LPOP, SQS ReceiveMessage with visibility timeout)
Visibility timeout / lease: task is invisible to other workers for N seconds; worker must heartbeat to extend
Acknowledgement: explicit ACK on completion removes task; NACK or timeout returns to queue
Task chaining: on_success callback triggers next task (chord, chain primitives)

patterns_reviewed.name: Temporal / workflow engine
key_primitives: Workflow identity: each workflow run has a unique ID; duplicate IDs are rejected or de-duped
Activity heartbeat: long-running activities heartbeat; server kills and re-queues on timeout
Signals: external events delivered to running workflows (equivalent to 'task received new info')
Continue-as-new: workflow spawns successor workflow on completion

patterns_reviewed.name: GitHub Actions / job dependencies
key_primitives: outputs: — job can export named values consumed by downstream jobs
Matrix strategy — fan-out over parameter sets

patterns_reviewed.name: Model Context Protocol (MCP)
key_primitives: Tools, resources, prompts — stateless request/response, no built-in task state
No native task coordination primitives in MCP spec v2024-11
Agents compose coordination on top via tool calls to external systems

new_fields.description: Identity of the agent currently owning the task. Set atomically on claim. Format: '<agent-type>/<instance-id>' e.g. 'claude/worker-3'. Cleared on release or completion.
When the task was claimed by an agent. Used with lease_expires_at to detect stale claims.
Absolute timestamp when the current agent's claim expires. Agent must heartbeat before this to extend. Server should re-open tasks with expired leases.
Last time the owning agent sent a heartbeat. Informational; server uses lease_expires_at as the authoritative expiry.

Task IDs that, when they reach 'done', should cause this task to transition from 'pending' to 'open'. Replaces the purely hierarchical parent_id for pipeline sequencing. Distinct from blocked_by (which is manual).

new_fields.type: SpawnSpec[] (under ext for MVP, promoted to core if stable)
new_fields.description: Task templates to auto-create when this task completes. Enables A→B→C pipeline. Each SpawnSpec has: title, description, tags, priority, assigned_to.
new_fields.note: Keep under ext.spawn_on_done in MVP; graduate to core after validation

new_fields.description: Client-provided key for create operations. Server deduplicates: returns existing task if key already seen within TTL. Allows agents to safely retry task creation after network errors.
Incremented each time a task is re-opened after a failed/expired claim. Useful for detecting retry loops and capping retries.

new_statuses.description: Task exists but is waiting for trigger_ids to complete. Not yet claimable. Sits between 'open' and the work queue.

new_endpoints.description: Atomically acquire a task. Server checks: task is 'open', no current unexpired agent_id. Sets status=in_progress, agent_id, claimed_at, lease_expires_at=now+TTL. Returns 409 if already claimed. This is the ONLY safe way for agents to take a task.
Extend the lease on a claimed task. Must be called by the owning agent before lease_expires_at. Sets heartbeat_at=now, extends lease_expires_at=now+TTL. Returns 409 if agent_id doesn't match.
Return a task to 'open' without completing it. Agent voluntarily relinquishes. Clears agent_id, claimed_at, lease_expires_at. Increments attempt.
Mark task done. Validates agent_id matches. Triggers spawn_on_done task creation and evaluates trigger_ids on pending tasks.

Return the highest-priority claimable task for the caller. Filters: status=open, no active claim, optionally filter by tags/project. Does NOT claim — agent must follow with /claim. Agents can poll this endpoint.
SSE or long-poll stream of task change events. Agent subscribes and receives events: task_opened, task_claimed, task_done, task_expired. Allows agents to react without polling.
new_endpoints.note: SSE is simplest; upgrade path to WebSocket if needed

new_endpoints.description: Existing create endpoint; extend to accept idempotency_key in body or Idempotency-Key header.

server_side_behaviors.name: Lease expiry sweeper
server_side_behaviors.description: Background goroutine (or lazy on-access check) that scans for tasks where lease_expires_at < now and status=in_progress. Re-opens them: status=open, clears agent_id/claimed_at/lease_expires_at, increments attempt.

server_side_behaviors.name: Trigger evaluation
server_side_behaviors.description: When a task transitions to 'done', server queries all tasks with that ID in trigger_ids. If all their trigger_ids are now done, server transitions them from 'pending' to 'open'.

server_side_behaviors.name: spawn_on_done execution
server_side_behaviors.description: When task completes, server creates tasks defined in ext.spawn_on_done with status=open.

server_side_behaviors.name: Idempotency dedup
server_side_behaviors.description: On POST /v1/tasks, if idempotency_key matches an existing task created within the last 24h, return 200 with that task instead of creating a duplicate.

transitions.trigger: direct complete (no claim required for human-facing flows)
POST /release by owning agent OR lease expiry sweeper

side_effects.in_progress: Sets started_at (if not set), agent_id, claimed_at, lease_expires_at
side_effects.done: Sets completed_at; evaluates trigger_ids on pending tasks; executes spawn_on_done
side_effects.pending → open: Fires task_opened event on watch stream

notes: pending is new — existing tasks without trigger_ids skip it and go straight to open
The claim endpoint is the only atomic path to in_progress for agents; PATCH status=in_progress remains for human/legacy clients but lacks atomicity guarantees
attempt counter increments on every open←in_progress transition

claim_loop.description: Standard pattern for an agent polling for work
pseudocode: if 409: continue  // another agent got it

watch_for_next_task.description: Event-driven pattern using SSE watch stream

setup: Agent claims and completes A → server opens B → agent claims and completes B → server opens C

parallel_fanout.description: Task A spawns B1, B2, B3 in parallel, then C waits for all three
setup: Create task C with trigger_ids=[B1.id, B2.id, B3.id]  (pre-create with known IDs)
Or: agent completing A creates B1/B2/B3 manually and creates C with those IDs
parallel_fanout.note: Pre-known IDs require UUIDv7 client-side generation. spawn_on_done is simpler for linear chains.

concurrency_control_recommendation.for_agents: Use POST /claim endpoint exclusively. Do not PATCH status directly.
concurrency_control_recommendation.for_human_clients: PATCH /v1/tasks/{id} with If-Match: <etag> header for optimistic concurrency (same pattern as beans).
concurrency_control_recommendation.etag_recommendation: Add ETag support to todo.open (hash of version+updated_at, or full record hash like beans). Return ETag header on GET. Require If-Match on PATCH. This resolves human-client race conditions independent of agent claim semantics.
concurrency_control_recommendation.claim_atomicity: Implement claim as a single transaction in the store layer: read current state, validate open+no-live-claim, write new state atomically. For JSONL store: use file locking (flock) around the rewrite. For future DB store: UPDATE WHERE status='open' AND (lease_expires_at IS NULL OR lease_expires_at < NOW()).

prior_art_worth_borrowing.borrow: Lease-based claiming with server-side expiry sweeper. Agents don't need to explicitly fail — missed heartbeat = automatic re-queue.
ETag-based optimistic concurrency on every mutation. Subscribe() event channel for watchers. blocking/blocked_by relationship arrays.
Declarative trigger_ids (needs:[]) for sequential pipelines. Job outputs passed downstream.
Heartbeat + activity lease pattern. Continue-as-new maps to spawn_on_done. Workflow identity dedup maps to idempotency_key.
If-Match / ETag for safe updates. 412 Precondition Failed on conflict. Agents already know this from web standards.

schema_diff.breaking_changes: Adding 'pending' to the status enum is additive. Existing clients treating status as open string remain compatible. Existing status transition rules need one new rule: pending→open.

implementation_order: 1. Add ETag support to existing PATCH (foundational, unblocks all agent writes)
2. Add agent_id + claimed_at + lease_expires_at fields to schema
3. Implement POST /v1/tasks/{id}/claim with store-level atomic check
9. Add pending status + trigger_ids + server-side trigger evaluation
12. Graduate ext.spawn_on_done to core after validation
